conversation.text: We are a bank. Can you handle high volume inbound calls? About 10,000 per day.

Yes, CINNOX is designed to handle high-volume contact center workloads. Our platform includes robust call routing and queue management built for enterprise-scale operations. For a bank at 10,000 calls per day, I'd recommend looking at our Omnichannel Contact Centre (OCC) or Ultimate CX Hub (CXHub) plans. Could I tell you more about how we route those calls?
How do you route calls to the right agents?

CINNOX uses Automatic Enquiry Distribution (ACD) to route calls. According to our documentation, routing modes include Simultaneous (all agents ring at once) and Fixed Order (priority-based assignment), along with skill-based, language, location, and time-based routing options. Source: CINNOX Documentation — Automatic Enquiry Distribution; CINNOX Feature List 2026.

Yes, IVR Menu is a confirmed feature in CINNOX. It allows callers to navigate through menu options before being routed to the appropriate agent or support group. Source: CINNOX Feature List 2026 (EN_CINNOX_Feature_List_v2026).
What plan would you recommend for our size?

For a high-volume banking environment with 10,000 calls per day, I'd recommend the OCC or CXHub plan — these are designed for full contact center operations with ACD, IVR, and omnichannel support. The DC plan is not suited for this scale. For a detailed capacity discussion and custom quote, I'd suggest connecting with our sales team.
